name: Neon Ukiyo-e
rationale: Edo-period woodblock flatness meets cyberpunk neon — the clockwork labyrinth becomes a Hiroshige-style cascading scroll, and the dragon's infinite spiral maps perfectly to the wave-repeat motifs of ukiyo-e, while electric teal and molten-gold fills give it the kind of high-contrast visual pop that stops an Instagram scroll cold in 2026.
preamble: Ukiyo-e woodblock print style fused with neon cyberpunk palette; flat bold outlines, no gradients, only hard hatching for shadow; colors: deep indigo night-sky background, electric teal (#00FFD1), molten gold (#FFB800), scarlet accent (#FF2D2D), ivory paper texture overlay; linework crisp as traditional Japanese hanga carving; mood is eerie stillness — the calm before catastrophic recursion; visual references: Hiroshige 'Sudden Shower over Shin-Ohashi Bridge,' Hokusai 'The Great Wave,' Studio 4°C Animatrix mechanical detailing, Lo-fi / synthwave album art with woodblock grain filter.
